title: rubus glandulosus var. pallidus
intro: Rubus glandulosus var. pallidus, also known as the pale-dwarf raspberry, is a lesser-known variety of the Rubus genus, characterized by its pale yellow or white flowers and edible fruits. The medicinal properties of Rubus glandulosus var. pallidus include its high content of antioxidants, flavonoids, and anthocyanins, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and anti-cancer properties. The health benefits of consuming Rubus glandulosus var. pallidus include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system, as well as exhibiting potential anti-diabetic and anti-obesity effects. Rubus glandulosus var. pallidus can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, which can be used to make jams, jellies, and other culinary products.
